The branch adds policy rule reference tables to the policy CLI docs and, because it is stacked, carries the tool-posture policy conformance source, tests, and docs from the parent policy PR.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a stacked policy/docs PR rather than a bug report. The review used the PR diff, current main source/docs, tests in the branch, and linked parent proof instead of a failing reproduction path.
commands.review_followup: Maintainer review should decide parent sequencing and policy contract risk; there is no narrow automated repair to queue.

target.title: Docs: add policy rule reference tables
target.body:
## Summary

Stacked on #85482. This docs-only follow-up upgrades the policy CLI page with a normalized field reference for every current policy category, including the new tool-posture section from #85482.

The goal is to make the operator-facing policy contract easier to review: each table maps a `policy.jsonc` field to the OpenClaw config or workspace evidence it observes and the reason an operator would use it.

## Verification

- `pnpm docs:list`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 docs/cli/policy.md`
- `pnpm docs:check-mdx docs/cli/policy.md`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/cli/policy.md`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Documentation-only reference coverage for existing Policy plugin rule fields.
Real environment tested: WSL checkout at `/root/src/openclaw-policy-doc-reference-tables` on Ubuntu-24.04.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran docs listing, MDX validation, Markdown formatting, and whitespace checks against `docs/cli/policy.md`.
Evidence after fix: `docs/cli/policy.md` now includes field-reference tables for channels, MCP servers, model providers, network, Gateway, agent workspace, secrets, auth profiles, tool metadata, and tool posture.
Observed result after fix: Docs checks and formatting checks pass.
What was not tested: Runtime policy behavior; this PR changes documentation only.
